items.title: Use log-space for numerical stability
items.avoid: Computing softmax then log separately in fragile manual loops.
items.prefer: Use log_softmax plus nll_loss, or framework CrossEntropyLoss on logits.
items.why: Log-sum-exp trick prevents underflow when q(y) is tiny and keeps gradients well-behaved.
items.example: PyTorch: F.cross_entropy(logits, target) instead of manual -log(softmax(logits)[y]) without stabilization.

items.title: Match loss to label type
items.avoid: Using multi-class cross-entropy with mutually exclusive labels when each example has multiple independent positive labels.
items.prefer: Binary cross-entropy per label (sigmoid + BCE) for multi-label; standard CE for single-label multi-class.
items.why: The implied PMF P differs: one-hot categorical vs product of Bernoullis.
items.example: Image tags cat and outdoor: train K independent sigmoid heads with BCE, not one softmax over tag pairs.

items.title: Report cross-entropy with units and baseline
items.avoid: Quoting raw loss without log base or comparison to H(P) or majority-class baseline.
items.prefer: State bits or nats, and compare to entropy of label distribution or uniform guessing.
items.why: Makes experiments comparable and shows whether the model beats trivial predictors.
items.example: Balanced 10-class problem: uniform Q gives −log(0.1) ≈ 2.3 nats; report val loss against that baseline.
